comment: Very cool problem typical Yosemite Valley heady topping out and stout high crux. Crux move is a Gaston on left shoulder

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Sentinel Boulders > B-1 Boulder

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Sentinel Boulders > B-1 Boulder

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > MSG Boulder
comment:
Nit boulder. 
Very good landing. 
In the shade first half of day. 
Very technical one move / body position problem. 
Skin friendly.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Camp 4 Boulders > Titanic Boulder
comment:
Attempt on second day. 
Feels very close and needs at least fresh skin from the right hand tiny crimp. 
Going with left heel in place feels more secure than toe but eventually you need to switch it to toe. 
Clean fall and needs small amount of padding. 
In the shade the full day. 
It is good to workout the dynamic version to go from start to left good hold with no intermediate.

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Atomic Mushroom Boulder
comment: Climbs very well topping is bit funky good spotters will make you feel safe.

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Atomic Mushroom Boulder
comment:
Climbs very well lots of fun on foot holds. 
Takes the shade afternoon. 
Needs 4 plus pads due to uneven terrain.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Highway 140 > Rift Boulder
comment:
wonderful gem outside of the valley 
very technical and needs good friction for my level :D. bottom flows okay can be better with knee pad but can be done without it. hard to execute the high toe. Think of when bumping the right foot the sequence is right foot bump right foot drop the hip bump left foot now bump right foot very high and rock over it.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Medlicott Boulders > Medlicott Boulder
comment:
First session went well. Needs good support on first moves. 
Four pads is minimum

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Medlicott Boulders > Medlicott Boulder
comment:
Good progress on second session. 
Needs good temperature for the first couple of moves. 
Need to execute faster on the beta. 
Tapping right hand index and middle finger can help.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > The Gunks > Steel Fingers
comment: Soft for the area

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Biddles > Biddles Tower
comment: A gem in the area probably the best at the grade.

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Biddles > Biddles Tower
comment: Pretty cool technical climb. Needs good left heel hooking

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Indian Rock Bouldering Area > Lower Indian Rock Area > Dark Side Boulders

comment: Great boulder good for solo sessions

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Klinghoffers

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Castle Rock Loop > Cave Boulder
comment: Last move is pretty tough and balancy. I couldn’t find a good way to get my right foot to stick.

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Indian Rock Bouldering Area > Nature Boulders Area > Nature Boulder
comment: Getting really closer to linking up this one. One new thing is to use an intermediate left hand before the last throw to right good rail makes the move easier than throwing from the left hand side pull

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > The Gunks > Steel Fingers
comment:
Good 6 attempts. Needs a fresh go and clean execution. 
First move is kind of high precision on right foot and good tension on the off foot. 
Once you hit the last good right hand crimp lock make sure the heel is well positioned kind of little bit to the left side to be able to load it up.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Pywiack Boulders > Pywiack Boulder
comment: Session 1 was able to do all the moves. Skin shredding especially pointer right crux move tape on next time.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Medlicott Boulders > Medlicott Boulder
California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Medlicott Boulders > Medlicott Boulder
comment: A wonderful technical gem

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Medlicott Boulders > Medlicott Boulder
comment: Need to work out the first move off the pad last move feels doable

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Pywiack Boulders > Pywiack Boulder
comment: Pretty straightforward when you get the right beta

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Pywiack Boulders > Pywiack Boulder
comment: Feels doable need to isolate the movement

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > The Gunks > Arete Boulder
comment: One great move very generous for a 4. Line is beautiful.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > The Gunks > Steel Fingers
comment: Send in second session. Flows pretty well and every move is very good one of the best v7 out there

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Tuolumne Meadows > * Tuolumne Bouldering > Sunrise Boulder
comment: Gets lots of love from the sun needs a good day to be in prime time. Very powerful and beautiful moves. Two little left foot holds help moving up while the heel is on. When going to big move can move from heel to toe feels better. Top out very technical but not very bad got to rock over like you really mean it

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Biddles > Biddles Tower
comment:
A gem needs lots of padding. 
Great session with James Ovi felt on the last stand up move. 
All the moves are doable. 
For the heel hook first need to use right foot to be higher and then make as much room as possible for the heel to be higher. 
It is possible to absorb the swing but better to try to use right foot to slow it down.

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Biddles > Biddles Tower
comment: Topping off is the crux the rest is a great gym style climbing

location: California > Joshua Tree National Park > Joshua Tree Bouldering > Central Joshua Tree Bouldering > Cap Rock Bouldering > Pumping Monzonite Boulder
comment: First move is very hard I only can do it 2/3 times out of 8 or 10 attempts. Need to perhaps find a more consistent way. next moves are at the level but very heady and needs more comfort Padding/spotters etc ...

location: California > Joshua Tree National Park > Joshua Tree Bouldering > Central Joshua Tree Bouldering > Cap Rock Bouldering > Sandy Wash Corridor
comment: still feels heady and needs more dedicated session

location: California > Joshua Tree National Park > Joshua Tree Bouldering > Central Joshua Tree Bouldering > Ryan Campground Bouldering > Moffat Boulder
comment: Pretty good route with cool challenges from stemming to heel hooks

location: California > Joshua Tree National Park > Joshua Tree Bouldering > Central Joshua Tree Bouldering > Ryan Campground Bouldering > Moffat Boulder
comment: Getting closer to the end of this puzzle I need to commit to the last heel hook. Note route is on the shade till 10:30 ish then it is fully baking in the sun until sunset

location: California > Joshua Tree National Park > Joshua Tree Bouldering > Central Joshua Tree Bouldering > Ryan Campground Bouldering > Flight Attendant Rock
comment: Pretty cool route with a couple of nice board moves

location: California > Joshua Tree National Park > Joshua Tree Bouldering > Central Joshua Tree Bouldering > Ryan Campground Bouldering > Flight Attendant Rock
California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Castle Rock Loop > Parking Lot Rock
comment: First session was great was able to figure out the best way for first couple of moves. Going up from the 2 hands match needs more refinement

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Castle Rock Loop > Parking Lot Rock
comment: Making some progress on this able to hit the last few holds but it still feels hard to move of it I need to make sure I can do that movement more precisely

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Camp 4 Boulders > Blue Suede Shoes Boulder
comment:
Very awesome climb at the level  
Good new beta first is the side pull to avoid the undercling and the second is the thumb catch when crossing. The start is hard to figure out but when you get good feet it is goes. Look for the lower left foot and tiny right foot low

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Camp 4 Boulders > Bruce Lee Boulder
comment: light attempt on the first move still feels very hard crimp and needs great conditions of skin and weather. first move requires great amount of hip opening flexibility to set the high left foot.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Camp 4 Boulders > Bruce Lee Boulder
comment: light attempt on the first move still feels very hard crimp and needs great conditions of skin and weather. first move requires great amount of hip opening flexibility to set the high left foot.

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Camp 4 Boulders > Blue Suede Shoes Boulder
comment:
couple of tries on this boulder went well almost got to the top last holds. 
- The most important move is the first going of very good right foot to a bad left foot rock over and grab with left hand a very clear incut left crimp. From there need to move right foot slight up by the center of the body followed by the left foot higher to grab a good left and right hand and go for the last couple of move. Beta for blue sued shoes  
￼ https://www.icloud.com/notes/08dWGneAkNsxMea0QOW8qzl4w#Beta_for_blue-suede_shoes

location: California > Yosemite National Park > Yosemite Valley > * Yosemite Valley Bouldering > Camp 4 Boulders > Blue Suede Shoes Boulder
comment:
yet another wonderful day on this boulder. the last move still not flowing and needs more work. Key learning last sessions are  
- most important when setting the heel hook need to maker sure it is weighted perhaps by releasing and moving the left foot before even crossing. Need to think more about how I am setting the heel and how i can use it to generate upward momentum to the next hold with the help guide of left foot.  
- the bottom part can be done via 2 ways heel hook or direct left hand up out of legs down below. 
- when going for the first crimp slopper you can use a sidepull rather than the undrecline rock over beta feels more direct and lest moves.

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Castle Rock Loop > Parking Lot Rock
comment:
Very hard to warm up on this climb on cold days. It is very sharp and burly on left hand. 
I need to get up and try the left foot again feels like it is a good idea where you can create good opposition

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Castle Rock Loop > Parking Lot Rock
comment: Very cool problem with a great last move. Key thing is to get the good left foot and use a thumb catch on right hand Gaston. It is a committing move that needs some work. The start flows well when you get right sequencing. I like going to the inside crimp right hand with a good left foot.

location: California > Eastern Sierra > Bishop Area > Buttermilk Country > Buttermilks Main > Cave Area > Soul Slinger Boulder
comment: Key beta to recall. When setting the right foot go for the first part of the hold and not the highest point that looks big. Using the second makes next move near impossible. The next pinch hold is great and when you get it don’t rush it

location: California > Eastern Sierra > Bishop Area > Buttermilk Country > Buttermilks Main > Cave Area > Soul Slinger Boulder
comment: Did about 4 good quality tries on this, felt so close but yet low percentage and needs better execution next time. ripped both ring fingers and left index was not far. good low temps needs to be there anything above 55 is too hot.

location: California > Eastern Sierra > Bishop Area > Buttermilk Country > Buttermilks Main > Drifter/Smoking Boulder
comment: Great climb first and last move pretty powerful and rewarding. Done after long day on Soul Slinger

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Indian Rock Bouldering Area > Lower Indian Rock Area > Dark Side Boulders
comment:
Still feels very hard even with great conditions almost as cold as it gets mid 40 to 50. Very hard on the right hand wrist there is only few tries till you feel it hurting.   
(+) Beta right foot needs to be looking/pointing outward right to get good surface and plenty of rubber when set correctly next move feels pretty doable. https://www.icloud.com/notes/036uMynUut8ziOWzn8hkiiqCA#Beta_for_right_Indian_man 
(-) I need to find correct way to link start to putting right foot on the spot. Next time I need to commit to the beta of Andy L skip heel hook and try to find inside right foot to first establish left and then get right up.

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Castle Rock Loop > Parking Lot Rock
comment: Trying this end of session feels okay no new high point

location: California > San Francisco Bay Area > South Bay Area > Castle Rock and Sanborn Area > * Castle and Sanborn Area Bouldering > Castle Rock Loop > Parking Lot Rock
comment: Getting closer on this one today found a good right foot hold to help getting the next move better check video with tick marks
